Cosmic Exploration is <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>an initiative wherein Disciples of the Hand and Land join forces to achieve interstellar travel.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

■The Basics
For this project to achieve liftoff, Searchingway has need of your artisanal skills. Objectives can be completed solo or in groups.

As the initiative progresses, your surrounds will be expanded with new facilities and areas to explore. <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Work together with the denizens of your Home World and watch your efforts pay off in real time!<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

To ensure the success of the Cosmic Exploration Initiative, contributors are asked to undertake a variety of different activities.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Stellar Missions<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Stellar missions enlist Disciples of the Hand and Land to leverage their specialist skills and provide materials and crafted items to help further the initiative.

You can confirm rewards and requirements of currently available missions via the Stellar Missions section of the exotablet.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Mech Ops & Red Alerts<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Mech op directives and red alerts are issued without warning to contributors in the field.

The former sees participants pilot mechanized walkers known as vacuum suits to undertake a variety of missions. Due to the technical nature of this work, these missions offer special rewards unobtainable through other activities.

Stellar missions are <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>requests issued to Disciples of the Hand and Land for the purpose of furthering the initiative.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

■Accepting Missions
Select Stellar Missions from the exotablet to view a list of available missions.
Select a mission from the list to view its location, rewards, and other details.

When you're ready to begin, select the Initiate button to undertake the mission. <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>You may only accept one at a time.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

■Duty List
Appears whenever a stellar mission is active. Heed the instructions to fulfill the request!

■Mission in Progress
During an active mission, select Stellar Missions on the exotablet to open the Mission in Progress window. Here, you may review all the relevant details of your mission.

■Mission Complete
Once you have achieved your objective, you may select the Report button from the Mission in Progress window to complete the mission and receive your rewards.
※For a higher payout, achieve a silver or gold star rating before reporting.
The Mission in Progress window displays details and commands relevant to your ongoing stellar mission.

■Cosmic Crafting Log
Exclusive to cosmic exploration, this crafting log contains recipes specific to Disciple of the Hand stellar missions.

Choose a recipe, then select the Synthesize button to begin cosmic crafting in much the same fashion as ordinary crafting.

■Stellar Reduction
A type of aetherial reduction performed upon materials acquired through Disciple of the Land stellar missions.

■Abandon
Select the Abandon button to forfeit an incomplete mission.

■Ratings
Upon completing a mission, you will be awarded a bronze, silver, or gold star rating according to the level of your success. This, in turn, affects the mission's reward payout as follows:

Bronze: No Bonus
Silver: 4x Bonus
Gold: 5x Bonus

Any mission completed with a bronze or silver star rating will henceforth have a checkmark beside its name in the mission selection list, whereas missions completed with a gold star rating will have a special symbol instead.
The cosmopouch is <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>a separate inventory reserved exclusively for cosmic exploration.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Mech ops are <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>special missions that occur at random intervals, utilizing powerful vacuum suits to help carry them out to completion.<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>

■Mech Op Directives
When mech op directives are issued, an announcement will be made to all players in the exploration zone.

■Participation
Players may take part as either pilots or ground support, each role having different requirements for participation.

Pilots are charged with <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>operating powerful mechs called vacuum suits in order to carry out these missions.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Each time a mech op order is issued, pilots will be selected at random from those who apply.

■Pilot Applications
To be selected as a pilot, players will be asked to submit a pilot application. These can be obtained from Alerot in Sinus Ardorum (X:22.4 Y:20.3) in exchange for lunar credits.

■Submitting Applications
Once a mech op has been issued, those seeking to serve as pilots may do so by accessing the Mech Ops Registration menu via their exotablets.
Those who possess an application will then be allowed to submit.

After the application period has concluded, those selected to be pilots will receive a notification.

A final verification prompt will then be displayed, after which pilots will board their vehicles and be transported to the mission site.

Please be advised that <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>any active stellar missions will be canceled.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

■Processed Applications
Applications are only processed and taken from players who are selected to be pilots. Please note that, once taken, they will not be returned should you later decide to forfeit your position as pilot.

Ground supporters are <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>another important role in the completion of mech ops, whereby players participate without the use of vacuum suits.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

■Participation
To join mech ops as a ground supporter, players must proceed directly to the mission site and select “Join Event.”

■Reaching the Mission Site
When a mech ops directive has been issued, players may speak with Alerot in Sinus Ardorum (X:22.4 Y:20.3) to be transported immediately to the mission site.

■Ground Supporter's Duty
Resources gathered during mech ops must be fed into nearby field scanners. When sufficient resources have been gathered, the field scanners will activate, revealing targets for mech pilots to strike.

■Special Rewards
As thanks for completion of missions as ground support, players who make sufficient contributions will receive a buff that increases the number of credits received when completing stellar missions.

Further details can be found under “Stellar Mission Rules.”

By assisting in the Cosmic Exploration Initiative, player contributions are recorded and tallied over regular intervals. These accumulated scores are used to rank players in the Starward Standings.

Players with the highest score at the end of any given ranking period are designated <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Star Contributor<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

Ranking periods will last twenty-four hours (Earth time), from <ResetTime>13</ResetTime><If(TopLevelParameter(218))><Switch(TopLevelParameter(218))><Case(1)>1</Case><Case(2)>2</Case><Case(3)>3</Case><Case(4)>4</Case><Case(5)>5</Case><Case(6)>6</Case><Case(7)>7</Case><Case(8)>8</Case><Case(9)>9</Case><Case(10)>10</Case><Case(11)>11</Case><Case(12)>12</Case><Case(13)>1</Case><Case(14)>2</Case><Case(15)>3</Case><Case(16)>4</Case><Case(17)>5</Case><Case(18)>6</Case><Case(19)>7</Case><Case(20)>8</Case><Case(21)>9</Case><Case(22)>10</Case><Case(23)>11</Case></Switch><Else/>12</If> <If(GreaterThanOrEqualTo(TopLevelParameter(218),12))>p.m.<Else/>a.m.</If> (Earth time) to <ResetTime>13</ResetTime><If(TopLevelParameter(218))><Switch(TopLevelParameter(218))><Case(1)>1</Case><Case(2)>2</Case><Case(3)>3</Case><Case(4)>4</Case><Case(5)>5</Case><Case(6)>6</Case><Case(7)>7</Case><Case(8)>8</Case><Case(9)>9</Case><Case(10)>10</Case><Case(11)>11</Case><Case(12)>12</Case><Case(13)>1</Case><Case(14)>2</Case><Case(15)>3</Case><Case(16)>4</Case><Case(17)>5</Case><Case(18)>6</Case><Case(19)>7</Case><Case(20)>8</Case><Case(21)>9</Case><Case(22)>10</Case><Case(23)>11</Case></Switch><Else/>12</If> <If(GreaterThanOrEqualTo(TopLevelParameter(218),12))>p.m.<Else/>a.m.</If> (Earth time) the following day. Once the Star Contributors have been selected, a twenty-four hour interim will follow before the next ranking period begins.
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>※In the event of server maintenance during a ranking period, players with the highest score in the available time before or after maintenance will be designated Star Contributor.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Player scores can be increased by undertaking stellar missions, or participating in mech ops and cosmic exploration projects.

After completion of the aforementioned activities, a system message will display in the log window noting the size of your contribution.

To learn more about these tallying periods or your present score, <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>speak with Scanningway at the Moongate Hub.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Players selected as Star Contributor will receive special accolades that last for fourteen days (Earth time) from the time they are chosen.

Star Contributors cannot be selected again for thirteen days (Earth time) from the day they are chosen. However, should they have the highest score on the fourteenth day, they can maintain their status as Star Contributor without interruption.
Players selected as Star Contributor will glow with a special aura while in cosmic exploration zones.

This aura will only appear when playing as a <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Disciple of the Hand or Land<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

Players may disable the aura by manually turning off the status effect “Astral Aura.” However, it will reset after leaving and re-entering the cosmic exploration zone.
Current Star Contributors can be confirmed at any time via the <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Starward Standings stela<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

The Starward Standings stela also displays a number of previously selected Star Contributors.

You can select any of their names to view a congratulatory message from the Cosmic Exploration Initiative.

Players selected as Star Contributor will also be commemorated with a <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>hologram of their visage in the cosmic exploration zone<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.
If you have been selected, you may change the pose of your hologram at any time by accessing the Starward Standings stela.

The Infrastructure Index allows you to <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>monitor the overall progress of the Cosmic Exploration Initiative as well as review entries in the development log.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

You can view the index at any time via the Infrastructure Index section of the exotablet.

■Confirming Progress
Information about the current infrastructure project and overall cosmic exploration progress is displayed at the top of the window.
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>※See the “Projects” section of this guide for further details about infrastructure projects.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

■Development Log
Reports on current and past development phases are listed on the left side of the window. Footage of each phase can be viewed via the Video Log button.

Projects are collaborative endeavors in which <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Disciples of the Hand and Land work together to complete tasks<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>, contributing towards the construction and expansion of various facilities.

Once the Cosmic Exploration Initiative has progressed to a certain point, and the necessary groundwork is complete, project <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>commencement timing<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> is determined. Details about preparation progress and project commencement timing can be found in the Infrastructure Index section of the exotablet.
The location of any projects currently underway is displayed on the map.

Depending on the project site and objective, participants will be required to perform various tasks, such as <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>transporting materials, fabricating parts, and removing obstacles<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

When the progress bar is filled, the project will draw to a close, and cosmocredits and experience points will be awarded based on <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>contribution<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

Successfully completing projects will transform the environment and further the exploration effort.

Stellar successes are milestones based on your contributions to the initiative. Various rewards are bestowed upon reaching these milestones.

You can keep track of your progress via the Stellar Successes section of the exotablet.

Stellar successes are divided into two categories: daily successes, which track your day-to-day achievements, and standard successes, which track your overall contribution to the initiative.

Participating in stellar missions and mech ops are just two of the many ways you can attain stellar successes and reap the rewards.

Beware that rewards received above and beyond your carrying capacity will be disposed of.

Daily successes reward <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>short-term contributions to the initiative on a day-to-day basis<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>. These milestones can be viewed under the Daily tab in the Stellar Successes section of the exotablet.

Completing daily successes awards Daily Success Points. Different rewards are available depending on the number of points accrued.

Daily success progress is reset each day at <ResetTime>13</ResetTime><If(TopLevelParameter(218))><Switch(TopLevelParameter(218))><Case(1)>1</Case><Case(2)>2</Case><Case(3)>3</Case><Case(4)>4</Case><Case(5)>5</Case><Case(6)>6</Case><Case(7)>7</Case><Case(8)>8</Case><Case(9)>9</Case><Case(10)>10</Case><Case(11)>11</Case><Case(12)>12</Case><Case(13)>1</Case><Case(14)>2</Case><Case(15)>3</Case><Case(16)>4</Case><Case(17)>5</Case><Case(18)>6</Case><Case(19)>7</Case><Case(20)>8</Case><Case(21)>9</Case><Case(22)>10</Case><Case(23)>11</Case></Switch><Else/>12</If> <If(GreaterThanOrEqualTo(TopLevelParameter(218),12))>p.m.<Else/>a.m.</If> (Earth time).

Standard successes reward <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>long-term contributions over the course of the initiative<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>. These milestones can be viewed under the Standard tab in the Stellar Successes section of the exotablet.
Corresponding rewards are given upon reaching each milestone.
The Cosmoliner is a convenient, high-speed transport network linking points of strategic importance across the exploration zone. Simply approach the warp gate to be whisked off to your destination.
As you work with other contributors to further the initiative, new sections of the Cosmoliner network will be added, providing swift access to more distant locales.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Data gathered during the course of the initiative supports cosmic research<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>─an endeavor dedicated to the pursuit of interstellar exploration.

As part of this research, analytical tasks are set for each Disciple of the Hand and Land class. Completing missions will procure the data necessary to complete these tasks and advance to the next research stage.

Details can be viewed in the Cosmic Research section of the exotablet.
When opened, the Cosmic Research Information window displays current research objectives for each class.

Once you have gathered enough data to complete your current analytical task, you can submit your report to Researchingway. The data-driven Loporrit will reward your hard work by upgrading your cosmic tool.

An icon will appear on the exotablet when you have sufficient data to report.

Designed for Disciples of the Hand and Land, <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>cosmic tools are the technological culmination of Researchingway's fastidious data analysis<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

As you complete analytical tasks, the ingenious Loporrit can upgrade your cosmic tool to ever-increasing heights of utility.

Cosmic fortunes are <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>one of several reward systems players will encounter while participating in cosmic exploration.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> Using credits earned through contributions to the initiative, you can draw fortunes to receive various prizes.

Contributions to the Cosmic Exploration Initiative are rewarded with credits based on the planet where they are made. In the case of Sinus Ardorum, players will receive lunar credits.

These credits may then be spent to <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>draw cosmic fortunes by speaking with Orbitingway, each draw costing 1,000 credits.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Mission Types
After paying the requisite credits, the cosmic fortune drawing will begin, and players will be presented with two roulette wheels.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Each roulette contains a unique set of prizes.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> After selecting one, the roulette wheel will be spun, and players will receive rewards based on where it stops.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>The roulette wheel has a chance to stop on a special marker, granting players a Stellar Opportunity.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Stopping on Stellar Opportunity will reveal a new roulette wheel with a new set of special prizes.

Players who make substantial contributions to mech ops may be rewarded with a ticket for a <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Big Bang fortune<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

Should you receive one of these tickets, speak with Orbitingway to test your luck on a special roulette wheel for a chance to win extravagant prizes.
Operating in volatile environs is not without its challenges. At times, unforeseen developments may call for emergency measures.

Whenever these hazards result in major obstacles to the initiative, the Moongate Hub will issue a <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>red alert<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>. Heed the call to arms, and work together with your comrades to resolve the crisis!
During an active red alert, you may aid in its resolution by undertaking <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>critical missions<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.
Depending on the type of emergency, some critical missions may only be available to certain classes. Whenever your specific expertise is in demand, you will be compensated handsomely for offering it.
During an active red alert, everyone must work together to resolve the most pressing issues at hand. Progress on that front is indicated by the alert gauge.

Each progress bar represents a different ongoing problem. Your objective is to ensure that <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>both are filled within the allotted time<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>. If successful, all contributors shall receive bonus rewards at the conclusion of the red alert.
While a critical mission is active, the classes in demand are listed to the left of each progress bar. Change classes as necessary to lend a hand wherever you're needed most!

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>So long as the timer has yet to expire, you are free to undertake critical missions, even if the progress bars are already full.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> Your reward will not be impacted.

For the Cosmic Exploration Initiative, there's no such thing as too many supplies, so don't hesitate to contribute if you have the time!

Critical missions may only be undertaken while a red alert is active. Upon its conclusion, you will be unable to complete the mission.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Additionally, once the timer falls below one minute, you will be unable to accept a new mission, as you will not have sufficient time to complete it.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Cosmocredits are a type of currency earned through contributions to the Cosmic Exploration Initiative. At the Credit Exchange, they can be used to purchase a variety of supplies and even exclusive wares!

■How to Earn Cosmocredits
Cosmocredits are primarily earned through the completion of stellar missions, but are also awarded through stellar successes and other avenues. Some tasks, such as construction projects, are more lucrative than others!

Though objectives, rating milestones, and other details may vary, each mission generally falls under one of the following categories:

■Stock Up on Items
Utilize your crafting or gathering abilities to collect a specific item. Upon reaching the requisite quantity, the mission is complete.

Disciples of the Hand may earn greater compensation by submitting high-quality items, whereas Disciples of the Land will be richly rewarded for items with a higher collectability rating. You can review the requisite milestones on the item help window.

■Race against Time
For these missions, the objective must be completed within the time limit, whether it's gathering a set quantity of items or fishing up different types of marine life. The faster you finish, the better your rating.

■Chains and Gatherer's Boon (Miners and Botanists Only)
Achieve as many successful chains and instances of Gatherer's Boon as possible. Upon achieving the requisite score, the mission is complete.

■Big Fish (Fishers Only)
For these missions, your rating is determined by the size of your fish. Some missions will evaluate your single biggest catch, while others measure the average size of your entire haul. Upon meeting the requisite size, the mission is complete.

Lunar credits are a type of currency earned through contributions to the exploration of Sinus Ardorum. They can be used to purchase cosmic fortunes as well as pilot applications for use in mech ops.

■How to Earn Lunar Credits
Lunar credits are primarily earned through the completion of stellar missions, but are also awarded through stellar successes and other avenues. During red alerts, complete critical missions for an even bigger payout!
Each submitted report will increase the quantity and variety of data collectable via missions─a key concern for the budding cosmic researcher.
Stellar Mission Rules
Cosmic Class Score

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Any materials or bait required for stellar missions will be placed in your cosmopouch free of charge upon commencement.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
The cosmopouch also holds your gathered resources, fish, and cosmic crafts. Upon completing a stellar mission, the relevant items are automatically submitted to the initiative.
The Exotablet
Zone Effects and Rules

When a red alert is issued, players are called upon to participate in critical missions, which must be completed within the specified time. For those who join forces and resolve these crises, generous rewards await.

Further details regarding both mission types can be found under the Mech Ops and Red Alert headings of this guide.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Completing Projects and Initiative Progress<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Once players on a particular World have progressed to a certain point, they can begin to undertake projects.

Completing these endeavors will greatly further the initiative and bring about a number of changes in the field. Given their importance to the initiative's enduring success, these projects offer lucrative rewards to those who lend their expertise.

Please refer to the Projects heading for more information.

The exotablet is <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>a HUD feature exclusive to cosmic exploration zones<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> from which you may review important information pertaining to every aspect of the initiative.

■Main Window
From this window, you have access to each facet of exploration, enabling you to accept stellar missions and check the overall progress of the initiative.

■Cosmocredits
Cosmocredits are a type of formless currency issued by the initiative and accepted at any interstellar base camp. The amount in your possession is displayed on your exotablet.

■Lunar Credits
Lunar credits are another type of formless currency issued by the initiative, but accepted only at the Moongate Hub. As with cosmocredits, the amount in your possession is displayed on your exotablet.

Players participating in the Cosmic Exploration Initiative are subject to a number of rules and effects.

■Traveling to Sinus Ardorum
Players may speak with Drivingway in Bestways Burrow (X:21.9 Y:13.2) to gain passage to Sinus Ardorum. Upon arrival you can travel between instances by speaking to Drivingway again in Sinus Ardorum (X:20.3 Y:20.0).

■Stellar Return
Stellar Return can be used anywhere within cosmic exploration zones. Upon use, players are transported to the rainbow crystal located in the center of the zone.

■Stellar Sprint
Within cosmic exploration zones, the rainbow crystal converts a crafter or gatherer's Sprint action into Stellar Sprint, boosting movement speed and effect duration.

■Mount Usage
Excluding the mechs ridden by select pilot candidates during a mech ops directive, mounts are not permitted within the exploration zone.

In the beginning, you are only permitted to undertake class D stellar missions. You will gradually unlock more difficult tasks upon reaching the requisite conditions, which can be found below the mission selection list.

There are a few special rules and restrictions associated with stellar missions:

■Quest Sync
The difficulty of crafting and gathering missions, as well as the amount of experience earned, will be adjusted to your current level at the time of commencement.

■Crafting and Gathering Restrictions
During a stellar mission, crafting and gathering will not award experience, and spiritbond will accrue at a different rate.

■Fishing Restrictions
At the end of each stellar mission, your Angler's Art stacks will be reset, regardless of how many you have accrued.

■Ground Support Bonuses
Ground supporters who make sufficient contributions to a mech op will receive bonus credits for a certain number of subsequent missions, regardless of objective. These bonuses are as follows:

Cosmocredits: +10
Lunar Credits: +10

For the duration of the effect, an arrow symbol will be displayed on the Initiate button on the mission selection window.

The cosmic class score refers to <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>the points individual players accrue by taking part in cosmic exploration, separated by class.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Whenever you complete a stellar mission, you will earn points for your chosen class. Reaching certain milestones will grant achievements and, in some cases, titles.

You may view your current score by selecting the Cosmic Class Tracker from your exotablet.
